metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full (model chip + context bar both render; bar from sidecar auto_compact_threshold) driver=gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full (model name AND context-utilization bar both render)
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
route: driver-gap

## Why this cell exists

1.8 is the first-contact visibility check (#599): after one trivial turn the dashboard must show WHICH model the session runs AND HOW FULL its context is. Acceptance requires BOTH halves — `metrics.model_name` non-empty/!=unknown, `metrics.context_window > 0`, and `metrics.context_utilization_percentage > 0`. This is a RE-ASSESSMENT: the cell was previously daemon=bug (model displayed but the context bar never rendered because mistral was absent from core/pkg/capacity's window map). Commit 4d3c2327 fixed it, so the daemon pillar is now full.

## Agent (yes)

Vibe writes a sibling `meta.json` sidecar next to every `~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/messages.jsonl`, rewritten after each turn. `config.active_model`, `config.auto_compact_threshold`, and `stats.context_tokens` are all populated from the first turn. Confirmed against the LIVE surface: sidecars carry `active_model: "mistral-medium-3.5"`, `auto_compact_threshold: 200000`, and `context_tokens` populated (e.g. 22418, 136662). The agent exposes model + context budget + context usage from first contact.

## Daemon (full) — model chip AND context bar both render

Model name: applySidecar (parser.go L132) sets `ev.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(st.model)` on every event; `NormalizeModelName("mistral-medium-3.5")` returns it verbatim (non-empty, != unknown). The `metrics.model_name` bullet PASSES.

Context bar (the fix): parser.go L138-139 now sets `ev.ContextWindow = st.contextWindow` on `turn_done`, where `st.contextWindow` is decoded from `config.auto_compact_threshold` (sidecar.go L72-73,L91) — vibe's OWN token budget before it compacts, the meaningful "context full" mark for a vibe session, read per-session so it stays correct if the user reconfigures it. This sidesteps the capacity-map gap entirely: instead of resolving the window from a (missing) capacity entry keyed on model name, the daemon takes the live per-session threshold as an explicit `ContextWindow` override. With a non-zero `ev.ContextWindow` reaching the tailer, `ComputeContextUtilization` (tailer_metrics.go) resolves `effectiveContextWindow > 0` and returns a non-zero utilization instead of the `(0,0,"unknown",true)` guard. Token count also lands (`ev.Tokens = TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens}` on turn_done). So `context_window > 0` and `context_utilization_percentage > 0` BOTH pass. metrics_test.go L52-53 pins `ContextWindow == 200000` (the sidecar threshold) as a regression guard.

Cost: the sidecar carries `stats.session_cost` + per-million prices, but the adapter does NOT emit authoritative cost — vibe's prices are user-configurable (a local llamacpp model is $0), so cost is a documented gap (the fix commit's own follow-up). The spec does not assert `cost_nonzero`.

## Driver (gap:wait_turn)

The recipe elicits the behavior with `send` -> `wait_turn` -> `sleep`; the acceptance is checked AFTER the first turn completes, so `wait_turn` is load-bearing. mistral-vibe's `driver-interactive.sh` still carries `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and SEAM 2 (`wait_turn`, L168-170) is a loud `not_implemented` stub, so `recipe-lint`'s `driver_elicits_from_file` returns only `send slash sleep` -> any recipe needing `wait_turn` is a semantic gap. SEAM 3 (resolve session_id + transcript path, L158) is also a TODO stub. This is tooling work, not an observability limit: `record` ports SEAM 2 (+ SEAM 3) from a reference driver (e.g. drive-claudecode-interactive.sh) before it drives.

## Route: driver-gap

daemon=full but driver=gap:wait_turn -> the routing table lands on driver-gap: `record` ports the missing `wait_turn` step (+ SEAM 3 transcript resolution), then drives. The recording will exercise the now-full daemon path (model chip + context bar), and the spec asserts that full behavior. No `known_failing` framing remains.
caveats: The daemon=full path is confirmed by reading the parser + sidecar + the metrics_test regression guard (ContextWindow==200000) and the fix commit's end-to-end note, but is PROVISIONAL on live emission until a recording — the sidecar is rewritten every turn and the tailer must pick up the post-turn context_tokens + threshold. record promotes provisional -> settled.

context_window is the session's auto_compact_threshold (vibe's compaction budget), NOT a published model window — deliberately so: it is sourced live per session and stays correct if reconfigured. context_utilization is context_tokens / threshold.

The driver gap is the operational blocker that must close before recording: wait_turn (SEAM 2) and transcript-path resolution (SEAM 3) are stubbed in the scaffolded driver — record ports them from a reference driver first.
Cost stays a documented gap: vibe's prices are user-configurable (local llamacpp = $0), so the adapter does not emit authoritative cost. The spec asserts model + tokens + context, not cost.
model_name is the agent's own normalized label; mistral-medium-3.5 is a concrete id (not a routing alias), so the 'auto counts' acceptance allowance is not exercised here.

sources.note: applySidecar sets ev.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(st.model) on every event; on turn_done it sets ev.Tokens = TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens} and, per the fix, ev.ContextWindow = st.contextWindow when > 0 — an explicit per-session window override that renders the context bar without a capacity-map entry.

contextWindow is decoded from config.auto_compact_threshold (raw.Config.AutoCompactThreshold -> st.contextWindow). This is vibe's compaction budget, sourced live per session — the meaningful 'context full' mark.
Regression guard: a sidecar with auto_compact_threshold:200000 + context_tokens:9000 yields SessionMetrics.ContextWindow == 200000 — proves the context-window bar now resolves for mistral models.

sources.ref: commit 4d3c2327 feat(vibe): render the context-utilization bar from the compaction threshold
sources.note: The fix for the previous daemon=bug: emit ev.ContextWindow from the sidecar's auto_compact_threshold instead of adding mistral to the capacity window map (the live threshold beats a guessed published window; the sample session ran to 136k > mistral-medium's nominal window). End-to-end tested through the production tailer.
NormalizeModelName("mistral-medium-3.5") returns it verbatim (no alias transform) — model_name is non-empty and != unknown, so the model-display half of the acceptance passes.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; SEAM 2 wait_turn is a not_implemented stub; SEAM 3 (session_id + transcript path) is a TODO. recipe-lint's driver_elicits_from_file returns only 'send slash sleep' -> driver=gap:wait_turn.

Live surface: sidecars carry config.active_model="mistral-medium-3.5", config.auto_compact_threshold=200000, and stats.context_tokens populated (22418, 136662, ...) — confirms the agent exposes model + budget + usage from first contact and pins the model id + threshold from the live surface, not docs.
Acceptance requires model_name non-empty/!=unknown AND context_window > 0 AND context_utilization_percentage > 0 after the first turn — all three now land for mistral-vibe post-fix.

preconditions: vibe CLI (v2.19.0) on PATH and authenticated (MISTRAL_API_KEY or ~/.vibe/.env)
~/.vibe/config.toml selecting a model (mistral-medium-3.5 observed live) with auto_compact_threshold set (200000 observed live)
irrlichd is recording with the transcripts permission granted
driver SEAM 2 (wait_turn) and SEAM 3 (transcript-path resolution) ported from a reference driver before recording (driver-gap route)
setup: Launch vibe in a fresh empty cwd so a new ~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/ is created
Assertions on model_name / context_window / context_utilization are checked AFTER the first turn_done
verify: events.jsonl shows one ready->working->ready cycle on the session
SessionMetrics token count after turn_done is > 0 (context_tokens from the sidecar)
SessionMetrics.ContextWindow after turn_done is > 0 (auto_compact_threshold from the sidecar)
